jobListings.jobDescription:
At ASIC, you can be the change that ensures a fair, strong and efficient financial system for the benefit of all Australians. Contribute to delivering on ASIC's purpose, vision, and strategic priorities to help maintain the integrity of the financial system and protect consumers from harm.

 

We are seeking a senior, highly credible Program Director to lead the delivery of ASIC's RegistryConnect digital transformation program. This is a critical leadership role accountable for delivering one of the Australian Government's Tier 1 digital transformation initiatives to modernise ASIC's business registers and related channels. Through the RegistryConnect program, ASIC will deliver reliable, secure, trusted and efficient registry services to support the economy for the benefit of all Australians strengthening trust in the financial system.

 

  • Location: Sydney or Melbourne preferred with other locations considered
  • Lead the delivery of a Tier 1, multi‑year digital transformation program of national significance
  • Deliver secure, modern and trusted business registers and related digital channels relied upon across the economy and uplift the quality and integrity of registry data
  • Remuneration commensurate with experience

 

The Role


Through RegistryConnect, ASIC is transforming and modernising its business registers to improve security, resilience, data quality and user experience for millions of Australian business, investors and consumers.


As Program Director – RegistryConnect, you will be accountable for the end‑to‑end delivery of this complex, high‑profile program, ensuring outcomes are delivered to scope, schedule and budget, while meeting rigorous whole‑of‑government assurance requirements.


Reporting to the Senior Responsible Officer (SRO), you will lead a large, multidisciplinary delivery function and work at the intersection of business operations, technology, policy, cyber security and regulation.


Key Responsibilities include:


  • Lead the successful delivery of the Registry Connect Program in line with the approved business case, benefits plan and Digital Transformation Agency (DTA) Tier 1 assurance requirements
  • Lead and coordinate large, multidisciplinary delivery teams and delivery partners, ensuring quality, consistency and alignment across all projects within the program
  • Define, maintain and govern the integrated program delivery plan, overseeing schedule, financial management, risk management and benefits realisation
  • Oversee program‑level risk, issue and dependency management, escalating material matters to governance forums and the SRO
  • Prepare high‑quality papers and reporting for executive committees, steering committees and portfolio governance forums.
jobListings.jobDuties:
About you


You are an accomplished senior program leader with a strong track record delivering large‑scale, complex digital transformation programs, ideally within government or highly regulated environments.


You bring:


  • Demonstrated experience leading the delivery of multi‑year, high‑risk digital programs with complex stakeholder and governance arrangements
  • Deep understanding of program management frameworks, assurance requirements and whole‑of‑government delivery expectations
  • Strong executive presence and the ability to operate effectively in complex, high‑pressure environments
  • Outstanding judgement, risk management capability and the ability to drive outcomes in ambiguity
  • Project or program management qualifications (e.g. MSP, PRINCE2, Agile) and experience working within Commonwealth assurance and procurement frameworks are highly regarded.

 

View the position description for more information or click ‘apply' to start your application. Applications close at 11:59pm AEST, Wednesday 20 May 2026.

 

About ASIC


ASIC's remit is one of the broadest of regulators across the world. ASIC regulates corporations, markets, financial services and consumer credit and monitors and promotes market integrity and consumer protection in the Australian financial system. Through our enforcement work, we hold to account those who contravene the law, working to achieve strong outcomes that address the greatest consumer and investor harms.


ASIC is committed to a providing a diverse and inclusive workplace where the very best talent in Australia chooses to work. Indigenous Australians are encouraged to apply as well as applicants from all backgrounds and with different abilities.


We offer a range of employee benefits including:


  • Attractive superannuation
  • Additional leave entitlements
  • 50/50 hybrid work-from home model
  • Flexible work arrangements
  • Assistance for study and professional development

Click here to view ASIC's salary and benefits guide.

jobListings.departmentDescription: The Future Fund is Australia’s sovereign wealth fund, investing for the benefit of future generations of Australians.  The Future Fund Board of Guardians, supported by the Future Fund Management Agency, invests the assets of seven public asset funds: the Future Fund, the Medical Research Future Fund, the Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Land and Sea Future Fund, the Future Drought Fund, the Emergency Response Fund and the DisabilityCare Australia Fund. Each fund has an investment mandate that is determined by the Australian Government under legislation.  Our role is to generate strong, risk-adjusted returns over the long term.
jobListings.jobDuties:
At the Future Fund, we’re for future minds – like yours. The new investors and creators who thrive on different thinking and doing it together. Those who glimpse opportunity before it strikes and step up every day to grab it.

 

The Future Fund is Australia’s Sovereign Wealth Fund, managing over $340 billion across seven public asset funds with the same purpose: growing Australia’s future. We are a global investor and work closely with world-leading investment organisations to access the best ideas.

 

Where others see challenges, we see more interesting opportunities. Every person, every skillset, every mindset is an asset. We’ve got every future generation to consider, yet our approach to investing is incredibly focused: we think deeply, and act deliberately. Ours is a culture where great people grow the future together — working collaboratively, thinking freely and making a real difference.

 

Are you Futureminded? Let’s create what’s next.

 

The Opportunity

 

We’re looking for a Manager, Investment Platforms to help shape the strategy, governance and delivery of the Agency’s third-party investment platforms within our Investment Technology function.

 

This is a high-impact role sitting at the intersection of Investments, Data, Operations and Technology — working closely with Investment Teams, senior investment leaders, and our DOT partners to ensure our platforms are well-governed, resilient, optimised and aligned to Investment priorities.

 

You’ll be part of a dedicated horizontal capability for third-party platforms, responsible for defining the platform target state, setting governance and operating models, co-managing vendor relationships (with IDM), and leading specialist delivery in a live investment environment. This role is reporting to the Senior Manager, Platform Strategy, and you’ll also lead up to two direct reports.

 

Key Responsibilities

 

  • Contribute to and execute the platform roadmap for third-party investment platforms, aligned to long-term Agency strategy
  • Work with Investment Teams and DOT to define, prioritise and sequence platform capability uplift (balancing local needs with scalable enterprise solutions)
  • Help establish and evolve platform governance, ownership and operating models (including initiative pipeline and roadmaps)
  • Act as a trusted advisor to Investment Teams on how to get the most out of third-party platforms to support decision-making and portfolio management
  • Partner with Investments to shape requirements and deliver fit-for-purpose platform solutions, ensuring a single, coherent engagement across DOT
  • Lead and coordinate delivery across strategic programs, business-led projects and BAU enhancements — with strong tracking, reporting and transparency
  • Ensure solutions meet non-functional expectations (security, availability, reliability, recoverability, usability) and are operationally supported once live
  • Drive strong governance discipline — ensuring initiatives are reviewed, endorsed and approved through the right forums
  • Build trusted relationships across Investments and DOT (including Investment Delivery & Platforms, Security, IDM and Investment Operations)
  • Maintain a clear view of emerging Investment platform and data needs, and translate them into prioritised action
  • Co-manage key vendor relationships with IDM, ensuring performance, clarity of accountability and alignment with Future Fund objectives
jobListings.jobEligibilityRequirements:
You’re someone who enjoys working to bring clarity, structure and momentum to a complex ecosystem of investors, data, platforms and delivery teams. You bring a practical, hands-on style, strong judgement, and the confidence to lead across stakeholders who are time-poor and highly expert.

 

You bring:

  • Significant experience delivering technology and data solutions for investment management teams, with a solid understanding of core investment concepts and workflows
  • Hands-on experience with enterprise investment platforms (for example Aladdin or similar), including configuration, data flows, integrations and supporting users in production
  • Strong capability in governance, prioritisation and delivery across multiple concurrent demands
  • Confidence facilitating workshops and aligning stakeholders across Investments and Technology
  • Strong relationship-building and communication skills — able to translate complexity into clear direction and practical next steps
  • Experience working with modern delivery approaches and integration patterns (data flows, system integration, user experience considerations)
  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Finance, Commerce or similar
  • Desirable: finance qualification (e.g. CFA)

 

We Are For Everyone

 

At Future Fund, we are better for our differences. Every person, every skillset, every mindset is an asset. We want everyone to feel comfortable bringing their authentic selves to work — which is why we’re looking for future minds like yours.

 

When doing your job means Australia has more money to invest in renewables, education, healthcare and medical breakthroughs, there’s a tangible impact to what we do. And we want you to be part of it.

 

Are you Futureminded? Learn more about us on our LinkedIn Life page.
jobListings.officeArrangementDetails: 3 days in office (Tue and Wed anchor days)

jobListings.departmentDescription:
Sport Integrity Australia leads the Australian government’s efforts to protect sport against threats to its integrity – doping, competition manipulation and the mistreatment and abuse of sports participants. Sport Integrity Australia is the cornerstone of the government's comprehensive sport integrity strategy: Safeguarding the Integrity of Sport.
Our role is to provide advice and assistance to counter the use of prohibited substances and methods in sport, stop the abuse of children and other persons in a sporting environment, cease manipulation of sporting competitions
and to protect members of sporting organisations and other persons in a sporting environment from bullying, intimidation, discrimination or harassment.

Sporting organisations, and other stakeholders, benefit from the ability to deal with a single nationally coordinated organisation to address all sport integrity issues. Sport Integrity Australia offers guidance on integrity matters to sports that don’t have developed integrity capabilities. Our focus is policy development, intelligence, investigations (primarily of doping cases), education, outreach and capability building. We are Australia's National Anti-Doping Organisation.

jobListings.jobCategory: Administration;Education, Training;Human Resources (HR) and People;Policy;Sport, Recreation, Tourism;Workplace Relations
jobListings.jobDescription:
Our Opportunity


This is an exciting opportunity to join Sport Integrity Australia’s People and Capability team in a newly established Executive Level 1 role with a strong mandate to design, build and embed workforce capability foundations.

The Assistant Director, People Development and Workforce Planning plays a strategic and hands‑on leadership role in strengthening organisational capability through contemporary workforce planning, learning and development, leadership capability and recognition frameworks. Working under broad direction, the role exercises a high degree of independence, sound judgement and professional expertise to deliver initiatives that support current and future workforce needs.

This is a role for someone who enjoys operating in complexity and ambiguity, translating strategic intent into practical systems, frameworks and programs that are still being established. While the role provides high‑level advice to senior leaders, it also requires a practical, delivery‑focused approach to building fit‑for‑purpose workforce capability solutions from the ground up.

The role partners closely with senior leaders across the agency to provide evidence‑based workforce advice, uplift leadership capability and embed sustainable people development practices aligned with APS employment frameworks and agency priorities.
jobListings.jobDuties:
In this role, you will:


• Lead and deliver strategic workforce planning activities, analysing workforce data, trends, risks and capability requirements to provide high‑quality, evidence‑based advice to senior leaders on current and future workforce needs, organisational change and priority initiatives.

• Design, build and embed contemporary workforce capability frameworks, including learning and development, leadership capability and rewards and recognition programs, ensuring alignment with organisational strategy, APS requirements and agency values.

• Translate strategic direction into practical, implementable people development initiatives, balancing long‑term capability uplift with immediate operational priorities in a complex and evolving environment.

• Lead and deliver strategic workforce planning activities, analysing workforce data, trends, risks and capability requirements to provide high‑quality, evidence‑based advice to senior leaders on current and future workforce needs, organisational change and priority initiatives.

• Develop and implement the agency’s learning and development strategy and overarching framework, aligning learning investment with organisational priorities, workforce capability requirements and APS obligations.

• Design, build and deliver contemporary learning offerings, including core capability, leadership and professional development programs, ensuring learning solutions are practical, scalable and fit for purpose in a developing environment.

• Lead the modernisation of mandatory and agency‑required training, reviewing existing requirements, uplifting content and delivery approaches, and strengthening governance, monitoring and completion processes.

• Establish and manage contemporary rewards and recognition initiatives that reinforce desired behaviours, performance and contribution, aligned with APS and agency frameworks.

• Manage the learning and development budget, ensuring effective prioritisation of resources, strong financial governance and value for money, while balancing strategic investment with operational needs.

• Manage complex projects and stakeholder relationships, exercising sound judgement, managing risk and delivering quality outcomes that may have agency‑wide impact, while communicating clearly and confidently on complex workforce and capability matters.

• Contribute to business improvement and innovation within the People and Capability function by identifying opportunities to strengthen systems, tools and processes, and by actively building capability through collaboration, coaching and knowledge sharing.

• Lead leadership and professional development initiatives that build skills, resilience and inclusive leadership behaviours.

• Evaluate learning effectiveness, drive continuous improvement and provide expert advice on capability uplift strategies to support performance, retention and future workforce readiness.

• Build strong working relationships with managers, employees, legal teams, unions, APS agencies and the APSC where relevant.

• Provide clear, confident and tailored communication to support understanding of complex issues.
jobListings.jobEligibilityRequirements:
Under section 22(8) of the Public Service Act 1999, employees must be Australian citizens to be employed in the Australian Public Service (APS).

All applicants offered employment will be required to successfully undergo a pre-engagement screening check, even if they have a security clearance. The screening check involves reviewing your background & personal circumstances to minimise risks in giving access to IP Australia information/resources. This screening check is conducted in accordance with the Australian Government Protective Security Policy Framework requirements.

The successful applicant must be willing to disclose all relevant and required information.
